description: A scene or place is 'nuelo' when little light reaches it. Before a color word, the adjective marks a dark shade, as in 'nuelo shilu' (dark blue). 'Nuko' (black) is a hue; 'nuelo' concerns illumination.
articulatory_notes: The tongue tip contacts the upper teeth for dental nasal 'n', and the lips round into high-back 'u'. True-mid front 'e' follows in hiatus and carries the stress. The tongue tip then meets the alveolar ridge for 'l' before rounded mid-back 'o'.
usage_notes: A dark night is 'nuelo shero'. 'Shomelu' (shadow) is the local shape made when an object interrupts light, while 'nuelo' can describe the condition of the whole scene.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a dim scene or a color seen as a dark shade
semantic_domains.physical: little light reaching a place, surface, or viewer
